title: Judge Approves Trump Effort to Obtain List of Jews From University of Pennsylvania
summary: The government’s effort to collect the names and phone numbers of Jewish people on campus as it investigates antisemitism has upset some people who worry about how the information will be used.

title: Judge Blocks Trump’s Order to End Funding for NPR and PBS
summary: The ruling will have minimal effect on the federal money going to public media because Congress voted to claw back funding. But it could have implications for any future funding.

title: Justice Dept. Struggles to Respond to Trump’s Suit Against IRS
summary: Officials at the department and the White House are in the middle of a messy and complicated debate over how to respond to President Trump’s lawsuit demanding $10 billion from the I.R.S.

title: Trump Seeks to Redefine ‘Regime Change’ in Iran War
summary: President Trump and his aides have made contradictory statements on whether the United States and Israel have transformed the Iranian government through violence.

title: Forgoing Oversight of Iran War, G.O.P. Calls Hegseth to Testify on Budget
summary: After resisting calls for public hearings for weeks, House Republicans have called the secretary of defense to testify at a budget hearing in late April for the first time since the attacks on Iran began.

title: U.N. Peacekeepers in Lebanon Were Killed by Roadside Bomb
summary: Members of the United Nations Security Council condemned deadly attacks on U.N. peacekeepers in south Lebanon and called for de-escalation between Israel and Hezbollah.

title: Stocks Recover Sharply Amid Hope for Iran War’s End
summary: Investors weighed the news that the owner of a Kuwaiti tanker loaded with oil said it had been hit in an Iranian attack near Dubai.

title: Will $4 Gas Hurt Trump’s Approval Ratings? Here’s What History Shows.
summary: Presidents since at least the Carter administration have seen their approval ratings tied to gas prices. But there are signs the correlation may be weakening.

title: Backdoor Funding of Homeland Security Agency Could Weaken Congress Anew
summary: An emerging Republican plan to skirt a Democratic filibuster and fund an entire department without congressional appropriations would be the latest example of surrendering power to the White House.

title: Who Is Getting Paid During the Department of Homeland Security Shutdown?
summary: At least 120,000 law enforcement officers who work for the agency have continued to collect paychecks throughout the funding lapse. But tens of thousands of workers have gone without pay.

title: A New Jersey Teen Finds Treasure, and More, in Abandoned Storage Units
summary: Michael Haskell, 17, set out to make some money from his locker dives. He ended up learning about life.

title: Supreme Court Rejects Colorado Law Banning ‘Conversion Therapy’ for L.G.B.T.Q. Minors
summary: The state and more than 20 others restrict therapists from trying to change the gender identity or sexual orientation of clients under 18.

title: TMZ Goes After Members of Congress Living It Up Amid DHS Shutdown
summary: After lawmakers left Washington for a two-week spring break with the Department of Homeland Security shut down, the Hollywood tabloid began publishing photographs of them living it up around the country.

title: Cesar Chavez Was a Voice for Mexican Americans Like Me. Now, We Grieve.
summary: The United Farm Workers co-founder had been celebrated as an exemplar of civil rights. Then, a Times investigation found extensive evidence of his abuse of women.

title: On What Was Once Chavez Day, Some Try to Highlight a Movement, Not a Man
summary: The reconsideration of the legacy of Cesar Chavez after he was accused of abusing women and girls has led some to question the lionization of icons, when reality is so often a letdown.

title: How Cesar Chavez Abused His Power
summary: The civil rights icon had a history of sexually abusing women and girls, which the Times reporters Manny Fernandez and Sarah Hurtes spent five years uncovering. They spoke to “The Daily” about their investigation, and one woman affected shares her story.

title: Gavin Newsom Suggests His Conservative Critics Are Gay in Online Insults Criticized as Homophobic
summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom’s aides said that their online insults were meant to ridicule figures on the right. But some critics say they are homophobic.

title: At the National Archives, the Declaration Gets More Company
summary: The Emancipation Proclamation and the 19th Amendment have been added to the Archives’s rotunda, the first permanent changes there in nearly 75 years.

title: Yes, This Is Your War, Too
summary: Even opponents of the conflict should want to see Iran defanged and defeated.

title: Trump Has a Way Out of the Iran War
summary: Trump should set aside his 15-point peace plan and reduce it to these two points.

title: Supreme Court Rejects a Ban on ‘Conversion Therapy’ for Minors
summary: Also, a judge halts construction on the White House ballroom. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.

title: What Was Your Mom’s Favorite Mantra?
summary: Share the go-to motto or oft-repeated bit of advice that has stayed with you throughout the years.

title: Tugboat Captain Is Charged in Miami Beach Crash That Killed 3 Children
summary: Six people, including a camp counselor, were thrown into the water on July 28 when their sailboat was struck by a barge that was being pushed by a tugboat, the authorities said.

title: Allbirds, Once Silicon Valley’s Favorite Shoe, Sells for $39 Million
summary: Despite once being valued at $4 billion, the company that made sneakers from Merino wool struggled to capture a wide customer base and turn a profit.

title: Four Tied to N.Y.C. Shelter Contractor Are Charged in Corruption Inquiry
summary: The arrests were part of an investigation that is also examining the conduct of a city councilwoman, her sister and the Brooklyn Democratic leader’s husband.
